猜想.摘要: Best-available closure keeps 'ICT' ahead of 'TICT', but repeated verifier-local limitations prevented further external shrinkage of the unresolved gap: The main gap remains exact-molecule ICT_vs_TICT closure: a discriminative rationale, ideally exact-molecule internal but at minimum highly scaffold-near, linking torsional distortion to state ordering and brightness strongly enough to justify why TICT is not dominant here.

最终证据摘要: Current top1 is ICT (0.57) and top2 is TICT (0.29). The latest evidence weakens the current top1 slightly rather than strengthening it, because the planned fresh microscopic discriminator did not execute: the round produced a capability failure, not new mechanism evidence. The top2 challenger TICT correspondingly gains a little relative weight because it was not further ruled down. An additional candidate, unknown, also rises slightly as residual closure uncertainty for the same reason, while neutral aromatic and ESIPT remain weak and unchanged. No still-credible additional hypothesis remains in coverage debt; portfolio screening is legitimately complete, so the case remains in pairwise contraction rather than reverting to portfolio screening. The key remaining discriminator is still an exact-molecule internal observable connecting torsional distortion to state ordering/brightness strongly enough to justify why TICT is not dominant here. The best next action is a verifier pass, not another microscopic request, because the latest round exposed a registry/capability mismatch for the desired internal route; external supplementation can now most informatively challenge whether any exact-molecule-near precedent or discriminative pattern reduces the ICT_vs_TICT closure gap or suggests a registry-compatible bounded task.

Best-available closure keeps 'ICT' ahead of 'TICT', but repeated verifier-local limitations prevented further external shrinkage of the unresolved gap: The main gap remains exact-molecule ICT_vs_TICT closure: a discriminative rationale, ideally exact-molecule internal but at minimum highly scaffold-near, linking torsional distortion to state ordering and brightness strongly enough to justify why TICT is not dominant here.

Verifier supplementation is already sufficient in breadth and remains mixed overall, but after the failed internal route it is now the best next step if tightly targeted to scaffold-near ICT_vs_TICT discrimination and to identifying a more registry-compatible follow-up.

Closure remains incomplete. Existing internal and external evidence still favors ICT overall, but the latest round did not deliver the planned fresh internal discriminator due to a capability block. Closure-building therefore needs either a sharper scaffold-near verifier discriminator or a newly specified registry-compatible internal task derived from that evidence.

Portfolio screening remains complete at round 14 of 20. All non-top alternatives are either directly screened or dropped with reason, so the case legitimately stays in pairwise contraction on ICT versus TICT.

Macro is not useful for the remaining excited-state pairwise question. Microscopic capability is currently the limiting factor because the most recent requested fresh-calculation route was not supported by the active registry, and repeated parse-only reuse had already plateaued. Verifier supplementation is already broadly sufficient, but after this internal capability block it becomes the best available next route if targeted tightly to the unresolved pair and to possible registry-compatible follow-up design. Recent working memory repeats the same verifier local uncertainty, so repeating the same verifier route is unlikely to shrink the current gap: Verifier evidence cards summarize external supervision but do not replace Planner-level synthesis; unresolved local gap: the evidence cards still need Planner-level synthesis before any mechanism decision.

Latest evidence is primarily negative planning evidence: the microscopic agent failed to execute the requested fresh internal action, reporting action_not_supported_by_registry for the hard-bound run_torsion_snapshots request. Therefore this round added no new exact-molecule mechanistic evidence, but it did clarify a real capability boundary that should shape the next move.
